  "concepts": [
    {"id": "c1", "name": "AI adoption in the public sector", "aliases": ["AI adoption in public administration"], "description": "The uptake and internal deployment of AI systems by public administrations.", "grounding_span": "the adoption of AI in the public sector", "location": "Introduction"},
    {"id": "c2", "name": "Public administration workforce", "aliases": ["public sector workforce"], "description": "Employees of public administrations affected by AI adoption.", "grounding_span": "the workforce in public administrations", "location": "Introduction"},
    {"id": "c3", "name": "In-house AI capability", "aliases": ["internal capability for AI"], "description": "Capability to develop and run AI systems within the institution rather than outsourcing.", "grounding_span": "Building in-house AI capability rather than outsourcing tasks", "location": "section: How can public institutions build AI capability?"},
    {"id": "c4", "name": "AI governance", "aliases": ["governance of AI"], "description": "Rules and oversight for the use of AI to prevent risks.", "grounding_span": "a proactive and robust governance of AI", "location": "Key messages"},
    {"id": "c5", "name": "Generative AI", "aliases": ["genAI"], "description": "Generative AI tools such as large language models used by staff.", "grounding_span": "generative AI (genAI)", "location": "section: What does AI adoption mean for the public sector workforce?"},
    {"id": "c6", "name": "AI literacy", "aliases": [], "description": "The ability of employees to use and interact with AI tools effectively and responsibly.", "grounding_span": "most employees will need \u201cAI literacy\u201d", "location": "section: What does AI adoption mean for the public sector workforce?"},
    {"id": "c7", "name": "Administrative and support tasks", "aliases": [], "description": "Core public-sector functions such as processing documents and managing claims.", "grounding_span": "administrative and support tasks", "location": "Key messages"},
    {"id": "c8", "name": "Human oversight", "aliases": [], "description": "A central principle for government procedures and services.", "grounding_span": "Human oversight, transparency, accountability and trust continue to be central principles", "location": "section: What does AI adoption mean for the public sector workforce?"},
    {"id": "c9", "name": "Transparency", "aliases": [], "description": "A central principle for government procedures and services.", "grounding_span": "Human oversight, transparency, accountability and trust continue to be central principles", "location": "section: What does AI adoption mean for the public sector workforce?"},
    {"id": "c10", "name": "Accountability", "aliases": [], "description": "A central principle for government procedures and services.", "grounding_span": "Human oversight, transparency, accountability and trust continue to be central principles", "location": "section: What does AI adoption mean for the public sector workforce?"},
    {"id": "c11", "name": "Trust", "aliases": [], "description": "A central principle for government procedures and services.", "grounding_span": "Human oversight, transparency, accountability and trust continue to be central principles", "location": "section: What does AI adoption mean for the public sector workforce?"},
    {"id": "c12", "name": "Data protection", "aliases": [], "description": "A risk area associated with unregulated genAI use by staff.", "grounding_span": "these tools can pose risks, for example, to data protection", "location": "section: What does AI adoption mean for the public sector workforce?"},
    {"id": "c13", "name": "Skills and capability gaps", "aliases": ["internal skills gaps"], "description": "Widespread internal AI-related skills gaps that hinder AI adoption.", "grounding_span": "Internal skills gaps related to AI are a widespread challenge in government institutions.", "location": "section: What does AI adoption mean for the public sector workforce?"},
    {"id": "c14", "name": "Digital and data professionals", "aliases": [], "description": "Specialists who develop, implement, and maintain AI systems.", "grounding_span": "digital and data professionals with advanced technical skills", "location": "section: What does AI adoption mean for the public sector workforce?"},
    {"id": "c15", "name": "Complementary skills", "aliases": [], "description": "Skills such as problem-solving and critical thinking.", "grounding_span": "complementary skills such as problem-solving or critical thinking", "location": "section: What does AI adoption mean for the public sector workforce?"},
    {"id": "c16", "name": "Transversal skills", "aliases": [], "description": "Social, management, or teamwork skills.", "grounding_span": "transversal skills such as social, management, or teamwork skills", "location": "section: What does AI adoption mean for the public sector workforce?"},
    {"id": "c17", "name": "Redesign toward citizen-centred public services", "aliases": [], "description": "Using AI as part of a redesign toward integrated, citizen-centred services rather than merely automating existing processes.", "grounding_span": "a redesign towards integrated, citizen-centred public services", "location": "section: What does AI adoption mean for the public sector workforce?"},
    {"id": "c18", "name": "Outsourcing", "aliases": [], "description": "Procuring tasks to third-party providers, one lever to strengthen AI capability.", "grounding_span": "outsourcing, hiring, and training", "location": "section: How can public institutions build AI capability?"},
    {"id": "c19", "name": "Hiring", "aliases": [], "description": "Recruiting talent, one lever to strengthen AI capability.", "grounding_span": "outsourcing, hiring, and training", "location": "section: How can public institutions build AI capability?"},
    {"id": "c20", "name": "Training and upskilling", "aliases": [], "description": "Investing in workforce skills development, one lever to strengthen AI capability.", "grounding_span": "Investing in training and upskilling", "location": "Key messages"},
    {"id": "c21", "name": "GovTech collaborations", "aliases": [], "description": "Strategic collaborations with start-ups, SMEs, research centres, or universities.", "grounding_span": "Strategic GovTech collaborations can help strengthen AI capability.", "location": "section: How can public institutions build AI capability?"},
    {"id": "c22", "name": "Targeted talent programmes", "aliases": [], "description": "Competitive programmes to attract and retain digital and data professionals.", "grounding_span": "Targeted talent programmes help attract and retain digital and data professionals.", "location": "section: How can public institutions build AI capability?"},
    {"id": "c23", "name": "Organisational culture of innovation and continuous learning", "aliases": [], "description": "An environment encouraging curiosity, critical thinking, and experimentation.", "grounding_span": "an organisational culture of innovation and continuous learning", "location": "section: How can public institutions build AI capability?"},
    {"id": "c24", "name": "Communities of practice", "aliases": [], "description": "A lever in the toolbox to foster innovation and continuous learning.", "grounding_span": "Communities of practice", "location": "section: How can public institutions build AI capability?"},
    {"id": "c25", "name": "Experimentation", "aliases": [], "description": "Spaces for experimentation in courses or labs to support AI adoption.", "grounding_span": "Spaces for experimentation in courses or labs", "location": "section: How can public institutions build AI capability?"},
    {"id": "c26", "name": "Large language model (LLM)", "aliases": ["LLM"], "description": "A model type; example is Gladsaxe's self-hosted GPT-based LLM.", "grounding_span": "a self-hosted large language model (LLM) based on GPT technology", "location": "section: What does AI adoption mean for the public sector workforce?"},
    {"id": "c27", "name": "General employees", "aliases": [], "description": "Workforce group needing to interact with and use AI tools effectively and responsibly.", "grounding_span": "**General employees** need to be able to interact with and use AI tools effectively and responsibly.", "location": "section: What training opportunities on AI does staff have?"},
    {"id": "c28", "name": "Leaders", "aliases": [], "description": "Workforce group needing a strategic understanding of AI technology.", "grounding_span": "**Leaders** require a strategic understanding of AI technology", "location": "section: What training opportunities on AI does staff have?"},
    {"id": "c29", "name": "Foundational AI skills", "aliases": [], "description": "Basic AI skills targeted at all employees.", "grounding_span": "targeting all employees to develop foundational AI skills", "location": "Key messages"},
    {"id": "c30", "name": "Strategic understanding of AI", "aliases": ["strategic knowledge on AI"], "description": "Leaders' strategic knowledge of AI and its potential and risks.", "grounding_span": "leaders to gain strategic knowledge on AI", "location": "Key messages"},
    {"id": "c31", "name": "Technical AI skills", "aliases": [], "description": "Technical AI-related skills for digital and data professionals.", "grounding_span": "digital and data professionals to improve technical AI-related skills", "location": "Key messages"},
    {"id": "c32", "name": "Trainer-led training", "aliases": ["trainer-led courses"], "description": "Trainer-led training, in-person or online, shown to be more effective than self-paced.", "grounding_span": "trainer-led courses, whether in-person or online, are more effective than self-paced ones", "location": "section: What training opportunities on AI does staff have?"}

  "definitions": [],
  "claims": [
    {"id": "cl1", "claim_text": "Kela, Finland's national social security institution, uses an AI platform to automate document classification and processing, saving an estimated 38 years of full-time equivalent work for case workers per year.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "saving an estimated 38 years of full-time equivalent (FTE) work for case workers per year", "location": "section: What does AI adoption mean for the public sector workforce?"},
    {"id": "cl2", "claim_text": "So far, AI adoption in public administration has been limited.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "so far, AI adoption in public administration has been limited", "location": "section: What does AI adoption mean for the public sector workforce?"},
    {"id": "cl3", "claim_text": "A lack of skills and internal capability is among the most widely cited barriers to the adoption of AI in the public sector.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "a lack of skills and internal capability is among the most widely cited barriers to the adoption of AI in the public sector", "location": "Introduction"},
    {"id": "cl4", "claim_text": "A significant proportion of public servants already use open-access genAI tools such as ChatGPT to support their work.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "Surveys indicate that a significant proportion of public servants already use open-access genAI tools such as ChatGPT to support their work.", "location": "section: What does AI adoption mean for the public sector workforce?"},
    {"id": "cl5", "claim_text": "Concerns that AI will worsen the quality of jobs or replace employees in the public sector are currently speculative.", "claim_type": "speculative", "grounding_span": "Concerns that AI will worsen the quality of jobs or replace employees in the public sector are currently speculative.", "location": "section: What does AI adoption mean for the public sector workforce?"},
    {"id": "cl6", "claim_text": "The municipality of Gladsaxe in Denmark operates a self-hosted large language model based on GPT technology.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "the municipality of Gladsaxe in Denmark operates a self-hosted large language model (LLM) based on GPT technology", "location": "section: What does AI adoption mean for the public sector workforce?"},
    {"id": "cl7", "claim_text": "The City of Helsinki's Experimentation Accelerator has to date supported 65 internal experiments with AI technology and new work methods.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "to date supported 65 internal experiments with AI technology and new work methods", "location": "Box 1"},
    {"id": "cl8", "claim_text": "In the EU, organisations that provide or deploy AI systems are legally required to ensure their staff has a sufficient level of AI literacy under Article 4 of the AI Act.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "organisations that provide or deploy AI systems are legally required to ensure their staff has a \u201csufficient level of AI literacy\u201d, according to Article 4 of the AI Act", "location": "section: What training opportunities on AI does staff have?"},
    {"id": "cl9", "claim_text": "Most institutions in the public sector lack a structured approach to identifying AI-related skills needs.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "Most institutions in the public sector lack a structured approach to identifying AI-related skills needs.", "location": "section: What training opportunities on AI does staff have?"},
    {"id": "cl10", "claim_text": "Trainer-led courses are more effective than self-paced ones.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "trainer-led courses, whether in-person or online, are more effective than self-paced ones", "location": "section: What training opportunities on AI does staff have?"},
    {"id": "cl11", "claim_text": "Governments have three main levers to strengthen their AI capabilities: outsourcing, hiring, and training.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "Governments have three main levers to strengthen their AI capabilities: outsourcing, hiring, and training.", "location": "section: How can public institutions build AI capability?"},
    {"id": "cl12", "claim_text": "Building in-house AI capability helps develop AI systems in line with institutional needs, prevents information asymmetries in procurement, and avoids dependencies on technology providers.", "claim_type": "normative", "grounding_span": "prevents information asymmetries in procurement, and avoids dependencies on technology providers", "location": "section: How can public institutions build AI capability?"}
  ],
